summary: Bug confirmed: SolanaExecutor.close() skips fee claiming by setting shouldClaimAndClose: false in buildRemoveLiquidityTransactions (line 113). This causes accrued trading fees to become unrecoverable on every rebalance cycle.
root_cause_hypothesis: The close action in solana-executor.ts (lines 107-114) calls buildRemoveLiquidityTransactions with shouldClaimAndClose: false, which skips both fee claiming and PDA closure. While a separate closePosition() call exists (lines 130-143), it only closes the empty position PDA - fees have already been forfeited at the removeLiquidity step.
gaps_found: The 'close' action path does not claim accumulated swap fees before removing liquidity
Missing documentation explaining why shouldClaimAndClose: false was chosen
new_bugs_risk: Option A (shouldClaimAndClose: true): Risk of transaction failure due to rounding errors, potentially blocking the entire close operation
Option B (Explicit Sequence): Lower risk but requires 3 separate transactions; fees may not accumulate if position is new
recommended_approach: Implement Option B (Explicit Sequence): 1) Add buildClaimFeesTransactions call before buildRemoveLiquidityTransactions, 2) Keep removeLiquidity with shouldClaimAndClose: false, 3) Keep closePosition call (already exists). This follows Meteora SDK best practices and provides better error isolation than Option A.
